grants.program: NRC Industrial Research Assistance Program (NRC IRAP)
grants.description: Advisory services and financial assistance for innovative Canadian SMEs to build innovation capacity and take technology-based products, services, or processes to market.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; project funding is determined with NRC IRAP based on the proposed R&D or commercialization project.
grants.deadline: Not listed
grants.fit_reason: Very strong fit for R&D on recycled-rubber formulations, new rooftop support products, improved product performance, automation-related process innovation, or commercialization of new technologies.

grants.program: NRC Industrial Research Assistance Program (NRC IRAP) – Financial support for technology innovation
grants.description: Funding and advisory support for Canadian SMEs developing and commercializing innovative, technology-driven products, services, processes, or other innovation activities.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; project funding assessed case by case
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for new recycled-rubber product development, testing, commercialization, and process innovation in Canada.

grants.description: Advisory support and possible contribution funding for Canadian SMEs developing and commercializing innovative, technology-driven products, services or processes.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; project funding is assessed case by case
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for new recycled-rubber product development, process innovation, testing and commercialization if the project has technical uncertainty and market potential.

grants.description: Cost-shared research internships that connect Canadian companies with post-secondary researchers to solve R&D, product development, process, or commercialization challenges.
grants.funding_amount: Standard model: partner contribution starts at $7,500 per 4- or 6-month internship, resulting in a $15,000 research award. Postdoctoral model can be $20,000 per unit.
grants.deadline: Not listed; contact Mitacs advisor before applying
grants.fit_reason: Excellent fit for applied research on recycled rubber, product testing, new materials, manufacturing process improvement, AI, or automation projects with a Manitoba academic partner.

grants.program: Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) Tax Incentives
grants.description: Federal tax incentives for businesses conducting eligible scientific research or experimental development in Canada, including deductions and investment tax credits.
grants.funding_amount: Most CCPCs may earn a refundable ITC at 35% on current and capital qualified expenditures up to the expenditure limit; other rates may apply.
grants.funding_type: Refundable / non-refundable tax credit and deduction
grants.deadline: Claim with the corporation income tax return for the year
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit if Clearline performs experimental work to improve recycled-rubber compounds, product durability, manufacturing processes, tooling, or automation under technological uncertainty.

grants.program: Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) Tax Incentive Program
grants.description: Federal tax incentives for businesses conducting eligible R&D in Canada, including deductions and investment tax credits.
grants.funding_amount: CCPCs may earn a 35% refundable enhanced ITC on up to $3 million of qualifying SR&ED expenditures annually; other corporations may receive a 15% non-refundable ITC
grants.deadline: Claim with corporate income tax return for the year
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for technical uncertainty work on recycled rubber formulations, product performance, new supports, or manufacturing process improvements.

grants.program: Regional Artificial Intelligence Initiative (RAII) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Funding for Prairie businesses to commercialize or adopt AI, including AI integration into business operations, productivity improvements and AI talent activities.
grants.funding_amount: $250,000 to $5,000,000 per project; up to 50% of eligible costs for businesses
grants.deadline: December 31, 2028, or until funding is fully committed
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for AI-driven production planning, quality control, forecasting, automation analytics or other AI adoption in manufacturing.

grants.program: Manitoba Research and Development Tax Credit
grants.organization: Province of Manitoba / Canada Revenue Agency
grants.description: Provincial R&D tax credit for corporations carrying on SR&ED in Manitoba; refundable treatment may apply for eligible in-house or institute-contracted R&D.
grants.funding_amount: 20% Manitoba R&D tax credit; unused credits may be carried back 3 years and forward 10 years; refundability depends on the type of R&D expenditure.
grants.deadline: Claim with corporate tax filing
grants.fit_reason: Strong companion to federal SR&ED for Manitoba-based product, material, and process R&D related to recycled-rubber manufacturing.

grants.program: NRC IRAP support for clean technology
grants.description: Support for Canadian SMEs developing, testing, or commercializing clean technologies with environmental benefits such as GHG reduction or improved air, soil, or water quality.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; includes funding to advance promising clean technologies from proof of concept through larger-scale projects
grants.fit_reason: Clearline’s recycled-rubber manufacturing and waste-diversion products could fit if the project delivers measurable environmental benefits and commercialization potential.

grants.program: Regional Tariff Response Initiative (RTRI) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Contribution funding for tariff-impacted Prairie businesses to boost productivity, adopt technology, diversify markets, and strengthen supply chains.
grants.funding_amount: Typically $500,000 to $5,000,000 per project; normally up to 50% of eligible costs. Up to $1,000,000 may be non-repayable for eligible businesses; otherwise normally repayable.
grants.deadline: Apply before December 31, 2027, or until funding is used; projects must finish by March 31, 2028
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit if Clearline can show tariff impacts and proposes automation, productivity upgrades, market diversification, or supply-chain resilience for its Manitoba manufacturing operations.

grants.program: Regional Artificial Intelligence Initiative (RAII) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Supports Prairie businesses and organizations to adopt, commercialize, or scale artificial intelligence solutions. Continuous two-stage intake through an expression of interest and invited full application.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; PrairiesCan received $33.8 million over 5 years for the initiative.
grants.funding_type: Contribution; repayable or non-repayable not listed on the summary page
grants.deadline: December 31, 2028, or until funding is fully committed; projects must be completed by March 31, 2029
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit if Clearline wants to deploy AI for production planning, quality control, sales forecasting, or product/service innovation.

grants.description: NRC IRAP support for Canadian SMEs developing, testing or commercializing clean technologies with long-term environmental benefits.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed; project funding is assessed case by case
grants.fit_reason: Good fit if Clearline frames recycled-rubber rooftop supports or manufacturing improvements as a clean technology or waste-diversion innovation.

grants.program: Business Scale-up and Productivity (BSP) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Interest-free repayable funding for high-growth Prairie businesses scaling up, commercializing technology, adopting leading-edge processes, improving productivity, and entering global markets.
grants.funding_amount: Minimum $200,000 and maximum $5,000,000 per project from PrairiesCan; up to 50% of eligible project costs.
grants.deadline: Ongoing expression-of-interest intake
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for manufacturing modernization, new equipment, automation, commercialization of recycled-rubber products, or export-focused scale-up if the company can demonstrate high growth.

grants.description: Interest-free repayable funding for high-growth Prairie businesses scaling up, commercializing technologies, adopting advanced technologies, improving productivity, or expanding markets.
grants.funding_amount: Up to 50% of eligible project costs; minimum $200,000 and maximum $5 million per project from PrairiesCan
grants.deadline: Continuous intake
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit if Clearline is scaling manufacturing capacity, adopting automation, commercializing new recycled-rubber products, or expanding into new markets.

grants.program: Business Scale-up and Productivity (BSP) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Repayable funding for high-growth Prairie businesses scaling up, commercializing technologies, adopting leading-edge processes and entering new markets.
grants.funding_amount: $200,000 to $5,000,000 per project; up to 50% of eligible costs
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for a Manitoba manufacturer investing in new production equipment, productivity technology, commercialization or export growth, if growth criteria are met.

grants.organization: Province of Manitoba / Canada Revenue Agency
grants.description: Provincial tax credit for qualified plant, machinery, and equipment used in manufacturing or processing in Manitoba.
grants.funding_amount: 8% tax credit for eligible property acquired on or after July 1, 2019; 7/8 refundable and 1/8 non-refundable for qualified property.
grants.deadline: Claim with corporate tax filing
grants.fit_reason: Strong fit for purchasing qualified manufacturing equipment, machinery, or energy-efficient Class 43.1/43.2 assets for Clearline’s Manitoba production facility.

grants.description: Supports Manitoba SMEs by reducing risk associated with commercializing innovative products and strengthening financial position for future funding.
grants.funding_amount: Not listed on located page
grants.funding_type: Provincial funding; details not listed on located page
grants.deadline: June 30, 2026 application deadline was formally announced
grants.fit_reason: Strong conceptual fit for commercialization of new recycled-rubber products, but the located official announcement indicates the June 30, 2026 deadline has passed.

grants.program: Manitoba Research and Development Tax Credit
grants.description: Provincial R&D tax credit for eligible Manitoba research and experimental development expenditures, administered by CRA on Manitoba’s behalf.
grants.funding_amount: 15% of eligible expenditures made after April 11, 2017; refundable for eligible contracted research with qualifying research institutes
grants.funding_type: Tax credit; refundable in specified cases
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for technical R&D on recycled-rubber formulations, product performance, process improvements or qualifying institute collaborations in Manitoba.

grants.organization: Government of Manitoba – Innovation and New Technology
grants.description: Cost-shared grants for Manitoba SMEs to commercialize innovative products or processes, including prototypes, tooling, testing, certification, market development, and commercialization scale-up.
grants.funding_amount: Up to $100,000 per project, 50% cost-shared.
grants.deadline: June 30, 2026 intake closed; no future intake deadline listed on page as of July 11, 2026
grants.fit_reason: Very strong fit for a new recycled-rubber product or process, but the listed intake deadline has passed, so monitor for the next announced intake.

grants.description: Tax credit for businesses acquiring qualified plant, machinery and equipment for manufacturing or processing in Manitoba.
grants.funding_amount: 8% tax credit: 7% refundable and 1% non-refundable
grants.fit_reason: Direct fit for a Manitoba manufacturer buying qualifying production machinery or processing equipment.

grants.description: Cost-shared funding for SMEs to support employee training, HR strategy development, curriculum development, and productivity-focused workforce development.
grants.funding_amount: 50% cost share; up to $10,000 per employee under Employee Training and/or up to $25,000 for HR Strategy Development; maximum $100,000 total funding.
grants.deadline: Training must occur in the current fiscal year; 2026-27 eligible training starts on or after April 1, 2026
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for upskilling production workers, supervisors, maintenance staff, or managers for automation, lean manufacturing, quality systems, safety, or productivity improvements.

grants.description: Repayable loans for Manitoba SMEs undertaking facility upgrades, infrastructure upgrades, machinery, equipment or IT projects that improve productivity, exports, jobs or workforce development.
grants.funding_amount: $250,000 to $5,000,000; up to 25% of total project costs
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for manufacturing modernization, production equipment, facility upgrades or IT investments at a Manitoba plant.

grants.description: Performance-based incentives and technical support for industrial, commercial, or agricultural facilities that complete electricity or natural gas saving upgrades.
grants.funding_amount: $0.25/kWh of annual electricity saved and $0.30/m³ of annual natural gas saved; incentives available up to 50% of incremental cost or amount required for a one-year payback.
grants.deadline: Not listed; pre-approval required before starting work
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for compressed air, pumps, fans, drives, process heating, waste heat recovery, ventilation, or other process equipment upgrades at a manufacturing facility.

grants.description: Funding for student or postdoctoral interns to help organizations address innovation-driven business, operations, process improvement, or market strategy challenges.
grants.funding_amount: $10,000 award with $5,000 partner contribution; or $15,000 model with minimum $7,500 partner contribution.
grants.deadline: Not listed; contact Mitacs advisor before applying
grants.fit_reason: Useful for market expansion, operations improvement, commercialization planning, export strategy, or data/AI adoption projects that need student research talent.

grants.organization: Government of Manitoba – Business, Mining, Trade and Job Creation
grants.description: Repayable loans for Manitoba businesses undertaking productivity, technology/equipment adoption, export growth, job creation, job retention, wage growth, or workforce development projects.
grants.funding_amount: $250,000 to $5 million, up to 25% of total project costs.
grants.deadline: Not listed
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for major manufacturing equipment, productivity upgrades, facility expansion, or export-market growth where repayable financing is acceptable.

grants.program: Canada-Manitoba Workforce Tariff Response Program - Employee Training Program
grants.description: Training support for Manitoba employers affected by tariffs or related market shifts to retrain or upskill workers and help retain employees.
grants.funding_amount: Up to 100% of eligible direct training costs; wage subsidies up to 100% of employee wages during training may be available where conditions are met.
grants.funding_type: Non-repayable training contribution and wage subsidy
grants.deadline: Continuous intake; training from April 1, 2026 is eligible
grants.fit_reason: Useful if Clearline can document tariff-related revenue decline, cost increases, lost contracts, or market shifts and needs workforce training to adapt.

grants.program: ECO Canada Digital Skills for Youth
grants.description: Wage reimbursement for young professionals in environmental jobs requiring digital skills such as programming, data analysis or website development.
grants.funding_amount: 80% wage reimbursement, up to $20,000; up to $25,000 for not-for-profit organizations
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for a digital or AI-related hire supporting production analytics, sustainability data, automation dashboards or e-commerce for recycled-rubber products.

grants.description: Federal wage subsidies delivered through funded organizations to help employers hire post-secondary students across Canada.
grants.funding_amount: Up to $5,000 for each student placement.
grants.deadline: Year-round applications through funded delivery organizations
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for hiring engineering, manufacturing, sustainability, business, data, or marketing students for defined projects or co-op placements.

grants.description: Supports Manitoba companies exhibiting or attending trade shows and missions outside Manitoba, plus market research and trade preparedness training.
grants.funding_amount: Up to 75% reimbursement for exhibitors; 50% for attendees; maximum $20,000 per event and $50,000 per company annually
grants.deadline: Open year-round until funds are fully committed for events between April 1, 2026 and March 31, 2027
grants.fit_reason: Useful for selling rooftop support products into new Canadian or international markets through trade shows, missions, and market-entry work.

grants.organization: Government of Manitoba – Business, Mining, Trade and Job Creation
grants.description: Cost-shared workforce program for Manitoba SMEs supporting employee training and human resources strategy development.
grants.funding_amount: Up to $10,000 per employee under Employee Training; up to $25,000 under HR Strategy Development; maximum $100,000 total across streams.
grants.deadline: Not listed; agreements cannot extend beyond the fiscal year ending March 31
grants.fit_reason: Useful for upskilling production, maintenance, quality, sales, supervisory, automation, safety, or technical staff.

grants.program: ECO Canada Employment and Wage Subsidies - Student Work Placement
grants.description: Environmental workforce wage subsidies for employers hiring students in STEAM or business roles connected to the green economy.
grants.funding_amount: 50% wage reimbursement up to $5,000 for eligible student placements.
grants.deadline: Not listed; funding is limited and employers are encouraged to apply early
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for hiring students into sustainability, recycled-material manufacturing, process improvement, engineering, quality, or environmental roles.

grants.description: Application-based support for companies expanding existing Manitoba operations or locating new operations in Manitoba, focused on training tied to job creation and expansion.
grants.funding_amount: Custom-prepared assistance; amount depends on net new positions, total project costs, company contribution, and Manitoba economic benefit.
grants.funding_type: Non-repayable contribution for eligible training-related expansion costs
grants.deadline: Continuous intake
grants.fit_reason: Good fit if Clearline expands production, adds net new jobs, and needs training, curriculum, or training-related equipment for new and existing employees.

grants.program: Efficiency Manitoba Custom Energy Solutions Program
grants.description: Performance-based incentives and technical support for industrial, commercial, or agricultural facility upgrades that save electricity or natural gas.
grants.funding_amount: $0.25/kWh of annual electricity saved and $0.30/m³ of annual natural gas saved; incentives available up to 50% of incremental cost or amount required to achieve a one-year payback
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for energy-saving upgrades to compressed air, pumps, fans, process equipment, ventilation, boilers, or plant-wide energy management in manufacturing.

grants.program: Federal Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) Tax Incentive Program
grants.description: Federal tax incentives for eligible R&D conducted in Canada, including deductions and investment tax credits.
grants.funding_amount: Qualifying CCPCs may earn a 35% refundable ITC up to the annual expenditure limit; 15% ITC may apply beyond the limit or for other corporations.
grants.funding_type: Refundable and non-refundable tax credit / deduction
grants.deadline: Filed with corporate income tax return; statutory filing deadlines apply
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for experimental development involving recycled-rubber formulations, performance testing, manufacturing-process uncertainty, or new product development carried out in Canada.

grants.description: Performance-based incentives and technical support for industrial, commercial and agricultural facilities completing electricity or natural-gas saving upgrades.
grants.funding_amount: $0.25 per annual kWh saved and $0.30 per annual m3 natural gas saved; up to 50% of incremental cost or amount needed for one-year payback
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for manufacturing energy projects involving compressed air, pumps, fans, refrigeration, process heating, ventilation or waste heat recovery.

grants.program: Regional Tariff Response Initiative (RTRI) – Prairie provinces
grants.description: Supports Prairie businesses negatively impacted by U.S., China, or Canadian counter-tariffs through productivity, competitiveness, growth, supply-chain, and market-diversification projects.
grants.funding_amount: Business projects typically range from $500,000 to $5 million; normally up to 50% of eligible costs; up to $1 million may be non-repayable for eligible businesses, otherwise normally repayable
grants.funding_type: Repayable contribution; possible non-repayable contribution for eligible businesses
grants.deadline: Projects must be completed by March 31, 2028
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit if Clearline can show tariff-related impacts and proposes automation, productivity, domestic supply-chain, or market-diversification investments.

grants.program: Export Support Program – Export Development Program (EDP)
grants.organization: Government of Manitoba – Business, Mining, Trade and Job Creation
grants.description: Supports Manitoba SMEs attending trade shows or trade missions outside Manitoba and related export-readiness costs.
grants.funding_amount: Event-specific reimbursement; airfare/baggage up to $5,000 per company per event, accommodations up to $3,000 per event, and other eligible export costs; program FAQ indicates up to $30,000 per fiscal year.
grants.deadline: Applications accepted for eligible activities between April 1, 2026 and March 31, 2027
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for selling rooftop support products outside Manitoba, attending construction or roofing trade shows, meeting distributors, or developing export markets.

grants.description: Cost-shared incentives for feasibility studies that assess technical viability, savings, and economics of industrial, commercial, or agricultural energy-saving projects.
grants.funding_amount: 50% of study cost, up to $20,000.
grants.deadline: Not listed
grants.fit_reason: Useful first step before investing in energy-saving manufacturing equipment, compressed-air improvements, process heat changes, or waste heat recovery.

grants.organization: Government of Manitoba / Canada Revenue Agency administration
grants.description: Manitoba corporate tax credit for qualified plant, machinery, and equipment used in manufacturing or processing in Manitoba.
grants.funding_amount: 8% tax credit historically, with 7% refundable and 1% non-refundable; from July 1, 2026 certain manufacturing and processing equipment receives a retail sales tax exemption instead of later refund, while 1% non-refundable credit remains for that equipment.
grants.funding_type: Refundable and non-refundable tax credit / retail sales tax exemption
grants.deadline: Claimed with annual corporate income tax return; purchase timing rules apply
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for qualified production machinery or processing equipment used to manufacture recycled-rubber products in Manitoba.

grants.program: ECO Canada Science Horizons Youth Internship
grants.description: Wage reimbursement for youth placements in climate change, environmental protection and sustainability roles.
grants.funding_amount: 80% wage reimbursement, up to $25,000, for placements lasting 4 to 12 months
grants.fit_reason: Good fit for hiring a junior employee for sustainability, recycled-rubber product development, environmental reporting or clean-manufacturing projects.

grants.program: ECO Canada Student Work Placement Program (SWPP)
grants.description: Wage subsidy for eligible Canadian employers hiring post-secondary students into environmental or sustainability-related STEAM or business placements.
grants.funding_amount: 50% wage reimbursement up to $5,000 per eligible placement; some priority placements may receive higher support where available.
grants.deadline: 2026–2027 placements announced; funding subject to availability
grants.fit_reason: Useful for hiring students for sustainability, engineering, production improvement, recycling, quality, marketing, or environmental projects.

grants.program: ECO Canada Student Work Placement Program
grants.description: Wage reimbursement for employers hiring post-secondary students into eligible environmental, STEAM or business roles.
grants.funding_amount: 50% wage reimbursement up to $5,000 per eligible placement
grants.fit_reason: Useful for hiring students into sustainability, recycled-materials, engineering, product testing, operations or business roles linked to environmental work.

grants.program: Manitoba Research and Development Tax Credit
grants.organization: Government of Manitoba / Canada Revenue Agency administration
grants.description: Provincial R&D tax credit for SR&ED carried on in Manitoba by corporations with a permanent establishment in Manitoba.
grants.funding_amount: 15% Manitoba R&D tax credit for eligible expenditures made after April 11, 2017; refundable under certain eligible-contract conditions with qualifying research institutes.
grants.funding_type: Tax credit; refundable under certain conditions
grants.deadline: Claimed with annual corporate income tax return; statutory filing deadlines apply
grants.fit_reason: Good fit if Clearline conducts eligible SR&ED in Manitoba, especially in collaboration with a qualifying research institute.

grants.program: ECO Canada Youth in Natural Resources
grants.description: Wage reimbursement program for eligible employers creating environmentally positive jobs in natural-resource-related sectors.
grants.funding_amount: 75% wage reimbursement, up to $20,000; up to $30,000 for placements in Northern, rural, or remote areas.
grants.deadline: Not listed; active employment program
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit for junior roles tied to environmental outcomes, recycled-material use, emissions reduction, or resource efficiency, depending on job and candidate eligibility.

grants.program: ECO Canada Digital Skills for Youth (DS4Y)
grants.description: Wage subsidy for employers hiring young workers into environmentally related roles with a digital component.
grants.funding_amount: 80% wage reimbursement up to $18,000 for eligible employers; up to $25,000 for not-for-profits.
grants.deadline: Not listed; apply through ECO Canada while funding is available
grants.fit_reason: Useful for hiring a youth employee for digital sustainability work, such as production data, emissions tracking, digital sales tools, AI/data projects, or environmental reporting.

grants.description: Refundable tax credits for Manitoba employers hiring eligible co-op students, graduates, apprentices and certain high-school vocational students.
grants.funding_amount: Commonly 15% to 25% of eligible wages depending on stream; caps generally up to $5,000 per student or apprentice
grants.fit_reason: Useful for hiring co-op students, apprentices or recent graduates into engineering, production, operations, quality or business roles.

grants.description: Incentives for third-party studies that quantify technical viability, energy savings and economics for potential energy-efficiency projects.
grants.funding_amount: 50% of study cost, up to $20,000
grants.fit_reason: Good first step before larger facility upgrades, compressed-air optimization, process heating improvements or waste-heat recovery projects.

grants.program: Efficiency Manitoba Strategic Energy Manager Initiative
grants.description: Supports hiring an on-site energy manager and provides coaching, training support, planning tools, and energy savings tracking.
grants.funding_amount: Financial incentives to offset salary of a full-time energy manager; specific maximum not listed.
grants.deadline: Not listed
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit if Clearline has enough energy use and projects to justify a dedicated energy manager for ongoing cost and emissions reductions.

grants.description: Tax increment-style incentive for significant new business investment or expansion in Manitoba, rebating incremental education property taxes generated by capital investment for up to 20 years.
grants.funding_amount: Rebate of incremental education property taxes for up to 20 years; requires minimum $10 million capital investment and other criteria
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit only for a major Clearline facility expansion or new property investment of at least $10 million with job creation or major economic benefit.

grants.description: Incentives for designing and constructing new energy-efficient commercial, multi-unit residential, or industrial buildings and additions.
grants.funding_amount: Up to $11.20 per square foot plus up to $12,000 toward energy modelling
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit if Clearline builds a new manufacturing facility or industrial addition and designs it to exceed energy-efficiency requirements.

grants.description: Refundable federal ITC for capital invested in eligible new clean technology property in Canada, such as certain clean electricity, storage, low-carbon heat and non-road zero-emission equipment.
grants.funding_amount: Up to 30% of capital cost for property available for use before 2034; up to 15% in 2034
grants.deadline: Available for eligible property acquired and available for use before 2035
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit for facility energy projects such as solar, heat pumps, storage, non-road zero-emission equipment or waste-biomass systems, if property qualifies.

grants.organization: Canada Revenue Agency / Natural Resources Canada
grants.description: Refundable federal tax credit for capital invested in adopting and operating new clean technology property in Canada.
grants.funding_amount: Refundable tax credit for eligible clean technology property; rate depends on property and timing
grants.deadline: Claim on corporate tax return or trust return
grants.fit_reason: Useful for qualifying facility decarbonization assets such as clean power, storage, low-carbon heating, or zero-emission non-road equipment, but not for ordinary manufacturing equipment.

grants.description: Financial incentives for commercial, industrial, and agricultural businesses that install eligible energy-efficient lighting and lighting controls.
grants.funding_amount: Amount depends on eligible lighting measures; customer bonuses may apply. Specific project maximum not listed.
grants.deadline: Not listed; apply before project completion as required by program rules
grants.fit_reason: Practical facility cost-reduction opportunity for a Manitoba manufacturer, though less strategic than process-energy or manufacturing modernization funding.

grants.description: Financial incentives for commercial, industrial, agricultural, institutional, and multi-unit residential building envelope upgrades, including insulation, windows, doors, curtain wall, and storefront systems.
grants.funding_amount: Incentive formulas vary by measure; customer bonuses available for projects completed before March 31, 2027
grants.deadline: Customer bonus applies to eligible projects completed before March 31, 2027
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit for reducing heating/cooling costs at Clearline’s facility during renovations, roof upgrades, or building envelope improvements.

grants.organization: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada
grants.description: Supports major innovative projects in Canada, including R&D, commercialization, business growth, large-scale investment, and collaborations or networks.
grants.funding_amount: Minimum SRF contribution is $10 million for projects with total costs of at least $20 million; maximum not listed.
grants.funding_type: Repayable or non-repayable contribution depending on project and benefits
grants.deadline: Not listed; consultation and eligibility process required before statement of interest
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit only for a transformative, large-scale manufacturing expansion, major clean-product commercialization project, or significant Canadian innovation investment.

grants.organization: Canada Revenue Agency / Natural Resources Canada
grants.description: Refundable federal tax credit for capital invested in adoption and operation of new clean technology property in Canada.
grants.funding_amount: Refundable tax credit for eligible clean technology property acquired and available for use from March 28, 2023 to December 31, 2034; rate depends on property and timing.
grants.deadline: Claim with tax filing; available for qualifying property to December 31, 2034
grants.fit_reason: Potential fit for facility clean-energy investments such as eligible solar, storage, heat pumps, non-road zero-emission equipment, or other qualifying clean technology property, but not for ordinary manufacturing equipment.

grants.program: ECO Canada Student Work Placement Program (SWPP) / Environmental Wage Funding
grants.description: Government-supported wage subsidy programs for employers hiring students or emerging environmental talent into environmental roles.
grants.funding_amount: Student Work Placement listed as 50% wage reimbursement up to $5,000 for eligible participants
grants.deadline: Not listed
grants.fit_reason: Useful for hiring students into sustainability, recycled-materials, product testing, environmental reporting, or process-improvement roles related to Clearline’s recycled-rubber business.

grants.program: Regional Tariff Response Initiative (RTRI) in the Prairie provinces
grants.description: Funding for Prairie businesses negatively affected by U.S., China or Canadian counter-tariffs to improve productivity, resilience, market diversification and supply chains.
grants.funding_amount: Typically $500,000 to $5,000,000 per project; up to $1,000,000 may be non-repayable for eligible businesses
grants.deadline: December 31, 2027, or until funding is committed
grants.fit_reason: Useful only if Clearline can show tariff exposure or direct trade disruption; could support equipment, productivity or market diversification projects.

grants.program: Efficiency Manitoba Commercial Energy Audit Program
grants.description: Incentives and technical support for eligible commercial building owners completing ASHRAE Level 2 or Level 3 energy audits.
grants.funding_amount: Up to 50% of audit cost; maximums vary by building type and audit level, up to $12,500 for Level 3 audits.
grants.deadline: Not listed; application must be approved before on-site assessment work
grants.fit_reason: Useful if Clearline owns or manages its Manitoba facility and wants an audit to identify envelope, lighting, HVAC, and other building energy savings before upgrades.

grants.organization: Government of Manitoba – Business, Mining, Trade and Job Creation
grants.description: Supports Manitoba companies locating new operations or expanding existing operations by contributing to eligible employee skills training costs.
grants.funding_amount: Custom-prepared case by case; amount considers net new positions, project costs, company contribution, and economic benefit to Manitoba.
grants.deadline: Continuous intake
grants.fit_reason: Useful if Clearline is expanding in Manitoba, creating net new jobs, and needs training support for new or existing employees tied to expansion.

grants.program: Worker Retention Grant for Work-Sharing Employers
grants.description: Grant for employers with an approved and implemented Work-Sharing agreement who provide training opportunities and want to maintain EI-eligible employees’ income during reduced hours.
grants.funding_amount: Varies based on the Work-Sharing agreement, number of participating employees, duration, and reduction rate.
grants.deadline: December 31, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET
grants.fit_reason: Conditional fit only if Clearline faces a temporary downturn, has an approved Work-Sharing agreement, and wants to retain and train employees rather than lay them off.
